title: L5_P4 (Chunk) Chunk 1
description:
[NULL] jumbled the intrusion log timestamps. Events are scattered across time. Without ordering, the forensic trail is useless. Implement the comparator, parse the entries, and format the sorted report.

Fill in the three tasks inside IntrusionLogger:
- TASK 1 (compareTo): Sort LogEntry by timestamp ascending. If timestamps are equal, sort by event name alphabetically.
- TASK 2 (buildLog): Parse each line as "timestamp:event" (split on ":"). Create a LogEntry for each and return the ArrayList.
- TASK 3 (formatReport): Sort the list with Collections.sort, then build a string where each entry is on its own line as "[timestamp] event". No trailing newline.

Input: first line is n (number of log entries), next n lines are "timestamp:event" strings
Output: all entries sorted by timestamp (then event name for ties), one per line as "[timestamp] event"

title: L5_P4 (Chunk) Chunk 2
description:
[NULL] scrambled the alert priority queue. Critical events are buried under noise. Severity must come first. Rebuild the comparator and sorter — severity descending, then timestamp ascending for ties.

Fill in the three tasks inside IntrusionLogger:
- TASK 1 (compareTo): Sort by severity DESCENDING (highest first). If severity is equal, sort by timestamp ASCENDING.
- TASK 2 (buildLog): Parse each line as "severity:timestamp:event" (split on ":"). Create a LogEntry for each.
- TASK 3 (formatReport): Sort with Collections.sort, then format each entry as "[severity|timestamp] event", one per line. No trailing newline.

Input: first line is n, next n lines are "severity:timestamp:event" strings
Output: entries sorted by severity descending (then timestamp ascending for ties), each as "[severity|timestamp] event"

title: L5_P4 (Chunk) Chunk 3
description:
[NULL] shuffled the incident categories. Events with the same name are split across the timeline. Grouping is destroyed. Rebuild the sorter — group by event name alphabetically, then show the latest events first within each group.

Fill in the three tasks inside IntrusionLogger:
- TASK 1 (compareTo): Sort by event name alphabetically. If event names are equal, sort by timestamp DESCENDING (latest first).
- TASK 2 (buildLog): Parse each line as "timestamp:event" (split on ":"). Create a LogEntry for each.
- TASK 3 (formatReport): Sort with Collections.sort, then format each entry as "[timestamp] event", one per line. No trailing newline.

Input: first line is n, next n lines are "timestamp:event" strings
Output: entries sorted by event name alphabetically (then timestamp descending for ties), each as "[timestamp] event"

title: L5_P4 (Chunk) Chunk 4
description:
[NULL] erased the log sequence numbers. Events exist but have no position. Without indexing, replay is impossible. Rebuild the sorter and assign sequential indexes after sorting.

Fill in the three tasks inside IntrusionLogger:
- TASK 1 (compareTo): Sort by timestamp ascending. If timestamps are equal, sort by event name alphabetically.
- TASK 2 (buildLog): Parse each line as "timestamp:event" (split on ":"). Create a LogEntry for each.
- TASK 3 (formatReport): Sort with Collections.sort, then format each entry as "#index [timestamp] event" where index starts at 1. One entry per line. No trailing newline.

Input: first line is n, next n lines are "timestamp:event" strings
Output: entries sorted by timestamp (then event alphabetically), each prefixed with a 1-based index as "#1 [timestamp] event"

title: L5_P6 (Chunk) Chunk 1
description:
[NULL] overloaded the traffic shaper's queue. Packets flood in with no limit, no order, no rejection. Restore the bounded queue — capacity is law, overflow means dropped.

Fill in the three tasks inside TrafficShaper:
- TASK 1 (enqueue): If queue size < capacity, add the packet to the END of the queue and return "QUEUED". Otherwise, increment the dropped counter and return "DROPPED".
- TASK 2 (dequeue): Remove and return the packet at the FRONT of the queue. If the queue is empty, return "IDLE".
- TASK 3 (status): Return the string "SIZE:current/capacity|DROPPED:count".

expectation.output:
QUEUED
QUEUED
QUEUED
DROPPED
SIZE:3/3|DROPPED:1
PKT-A
SIZE:2/3|DROPPED:1

title: L5_P6 (Chunk) Chunk 2
description:
[NULL] disabled the traffic meter. Packets flow but nothing tracks throughput. The processed count is always zero. Restore the queue and track every successful dequeue.

Fill in the three tasks inside TrafficShaper:
- TASK 1 (enqueue): If queue size < capacity, add packet to the END and return "QUEUED". Otherwise return "DROPPED" (do NOT increment anything).
- TASK 2 (dequeue): Remove and return the front packet, and increment the processed counter. If queue is empty, return "IDLE" (do NOT increment processed).
- TASK 3 (status): Return the string "SIZE:current/capacity|PROCESSED:count".

java.name: Java Template

expectation.output:
QUEUED
QUEUED
QUEUED
PKT-A
PKT-B
SIZE:1/3|PROCESSED:2

title: L5_P6 (Chunk) Chunk 3
description:
[NULL] broke the priority lane in the traffic shaper. VIP packets wait in line like everyone else. The fast lane is dead. Restore priority enqueue — VIP goes to the front, normal to the back.

Fill in the three tasks inside TrafficShaper:
- TASK 1 (enqueue with priority flag): If queue size < capacity, add the packet to the FRONT if priority is true (return "PRIORITY_QUEUED") or to the END if priority is false (return "QUEUED"). If full, increment dropped and return "DROPPED".
- TASK 2 (dequeue): Remove and return the front packet. Return "IDLE" if empty.
- TASK 3 (status): Return "SIZE:current/capacity|DROPPED:count".

java.name: Java Template

title: L5_P6 (Chunk) Chunk 4
description:
[NULL] wiped the traffic shaper's inspection tools. Packets enter the queue but there is no way to inspect them without consuming. Restore the queue with non-destructive peek and lifetime tracking.

Fill in the three tasks inside TrafficShaper:
- TASK 1 (enqueue): If queue size < capacity, add packet to the END, increment totalEnqueued, and return "QUEUED". If full, return "DROPPED" (do NOT increment totalEnqueued).
- TASK 2 (peek): Return the packet at the FRONT without removing it. Return "IDLE" if empty.
- TASK 3 (status): Return "SIZE:current/capacity|TOTAL:totalEnqueued".
